title: Getting started with cpaic
text: Network meta-analysis needs connected network. evidence splits two sub-networks common comparator, network disconnected treatments different sub-networks compared directly. cpaic reconnects network additive component structure treatments (component network meta-analysis), adjusts comparisons -study differences effect modifiers using anchored population-adjustment methods (STC, MAIC, ML-NMR). result indirect comparison connected population-adjusted.

title: Getting started with cpaic
text: bundled data describe binary-outcome network two pieces: sub-network 1, anchored placebo: Placebo, , B; sub-network 2, isolated: +B, +B+C, +B+D. treatment shared two pieces, network disconnected. shared components B bridge .
code: net <- cpaic_network(cpaic_bin_agd, ipd = cpaic_bin_ipd, sm = "OR",                      family = "binomial", ipd_covariates = "x1",                      inactive = "Placebo") net #> cpaic component network #>   Summary measure:   OR #>   Treatments:        6 #>   Components:        4 (A, B, C, D) #>   AgD comparisons:   5 #>   Reference:         Placebo #>   Inactive:          Placebo #>   IPD studies:       2 (binomial; 3200 patients) #>   Connected:         FALSE | components bridgeable: TRUE

title: Getting started with cpaic
text: disconnected network can bridged shared components make component effects identifiable, rank(X) = number components. report confirms two sub-networks, identifies B bridging components, shows component effects identifiable.
code: cpaic_connectivity(net) #> cpaic connectivity #>   Connected network: FALSE #>   Sub-networks:      2 #>     [1] 3 treatments #>     [2] 3 treatments #>   Bridging components: A, B #>   Component design:  rank(X) = 4 / 4 components -> all component effects identified #>   Estimable effects: 5 / 5 vs Placebo plot(net)

title: Getting started with cpaic
text: cnma_bridge() fits additive component model reconstructs relative effects across gap.

title: Getting started with cpaic
text: Components C D come IPD studies, whose effect modifier x1 imbalanced relative target population (x1 = 0). Anchored STC fits outcome regression treatment--x1 interactions reads treatment effect target. Anchored MAIC instead reweights IPD study target population. Population adjustment moves C D effects relative unadjusted (naive) bridge, placebo-anchored components B unchanged:
code: fit_stc <- cstc(net, target = c(x1 = 0), effect_modifiers = "x1") component_effects(fit_stc) #>   component  estimate        se        lower     upper statistic        pval #> 1         A 0.5000000 0.2563324 -0.002402322 1.0024023  1.950592 0.051105590 #> 2         B 0.4000000 0.2563324 -0.102402322 0.9024023  1.560474 0.118647988 #> 3         C 0.4896667 0.2406290  0.018042458 0.9612910  2.034944 0.041856471 #> 4         D 0.6408956 0.2317142  0.186744196 1.0950470  2.765889 0.005676788 fit_maic <- cmaic(net, target = c(x1 = 0), effect_modifiers = "x1",                   n_boot = 100, seed = 1) effective_sample_size(fit_maic) #>       S3       S4  #> 207.4202 358.1461 data.frame(   component = component_effects(br)$component,   naive     = round(component_effects(br)$estimate, 3),   cSTC      = round(component_effects(fit_stc)$estimate, 3),   cMAIC     = round(component_effects(fit_maic)$estimate, 3) ) #>   component naive  cSTC cMAIC #> 1         A 0.500 0.500 0.500 #> 2         B 0.400 0.400 0.400 #> 3         C 0.717 0.490 0.697 #> 4         D 0.325 0.641 0.772

title: Getting started with cpaic
code: relative_effects(fit_stc) #> Relative effects (OR, back-transformed) #>  treatment comparator estimate    se lower  upper     z     p #>          A    Placebo    1.649 0.256 0.998  2.725 1.951 0.051 #>        A+B    Placebo    2.460 0.363 1.209  5.005 2.483 0.013 #>      A+B+C    Placebo    4.014 0.435 1.711  9.416 3.194 0.001 #>      A+B+D    Placebo    4.669 0.430 2.009 10.850 3.582 0.000 #>          B    Placebo    1.492 0.256 0.903  2.466 1.560 0.119 additivity_test(fit_stc) #> Additive component model: fit statistics #>   Total lack of fit (Q.additive): Q = 2.669, df = 1, p = 0.102 #>   Additivity restrictions (Q.diff): not available -- no standard NMA #>     is estimable on a disconnected network. #>   Note: neither statistic tests whether component effects are constant #>   ACROSS sub-networks, which is the assumption that bridges the gap. #>   That assumption is untestable from the data and must be justified #>   clinically. forest(fit_stc)

title: Getting started with cpaic
text: vignette("cpaic-methods") covers statistical framework depth. full mathematical foundations validation study provided development sources.

title: The cpaic statistical framework
text: cpaic targets networks disconnected: treatments split two sub-networks common comparator, standard network meta-analysis compare across gap. Two layers solve two distinct problems. Connection layer (component network meta-analysis). Multi-component treatments decomposed additive component effects. sub-networks share components, component effects bridge gap. Adjustment layer (population adjustment). individual patient data (IPD) available, evidence edge corrected -study imbalance effect modifiers, using anchored STC, MAIC, ML-NMR. output indirect comparison connected population-adjusted.

title: The cpaic statistical framework
text: Let δ\delta vector observed relative effects (one per comparison), BB edge-incidence (contrast) matrix mapping comparisons treatments, CC treatment--component matrix Ctc=1C_{tc} = 1 treatment tt contains component cc. Treatment effects additive component effects β\beta, θ=Cβ,δ=Bθ=BCβ=Xβ, \theta = C\beta, \qquad \delta = B\theta = BC\beta = X\beta,  X=BCX = BC component design matrix. inverse-variance weights WW, component effects estimated weighted least squares β̂=(X⊤WX)+X⊤Wd,Cov(θ̂)=C(X⊤WX)+C⊤, \hat\beta = (X^\top W X)^{+} X^\top W d, \qquad    \mathrm{Cov}(\hat\theta) = C (X^\top W X)^{+} C^\top,  (⋅)+(\cdot)^{+} Moore-Penrose inverse dd data vector (Rücker et al. 2020). additivity assumption checked Cochran QQ statistic. implemented cnma_bridge(), wrapper around netmeta::discomb().

title: The cpaic statistical framework
text: disconnected network can bridged shared components make component effects identifiable, rank(X)=K\mathrm{rank}(X) = K, number components. cpaic_connectivity() detects sub-networks, lists bridging components, reports identifiability. identifiable, cnma_bridge() reconstructs relative effects across gap component effects.
code: net <- cpaic_network(cpaic_bin_agd, sm = "OR", inactive = "Placebo") cpaic_connectivity(net) #> cpaic connectivity #>   Connected network: FALSE #>   Sub-networks:      2 #>     [1] 3 treatments #>     [2] 3 treatments #>   Bridging components: A, B #>   Component design:  rank(X) = 4 / 4 components -> all component effects identified #>   Estimable effects: 5 / 5 vs Placebo component_effects(cnma_bridge(net)) #>   component  estimate        se     lower    upper statistic      pval #> 1         A 0.5000000 1.1922140 -1.836697 2.836697 0.4193878 0.6749328 #> 2         B 0.4000000 1.1922140 -1.936697 2.736697 0.3355102 0.7372402 #> 3         C 0.7170248 0.9734562 -1.190914 2.624964 0.7365763 0.4613800 #> 4         D 0.3250136 0.9728622 -1.581761 2.231788 0.3340798 0.7383193

title: The cpaic statistical framework
text: IPD study, cstc() fits outcome regression treatment main effects, prognostic main effects, treatment--effect-modifier interactions, effect modifiers centered target population. link scale, g{E(y∣arm t,x)}=μ+βt+γt⊤(x−x‾target)+… g\{E(y \mid \text{arm } t, x)\}    = \mu + \beta_t + \gamma_t^\top (x - \bar x_{\text{target}}) + \dots  treatment coefficient βt\beta_t population-adjusted contrast target (interaction term vanishes x=x‾targetx = \bar x_{\text{target}}). anchored generalization regression-based standardization; implemented natively mlumr::stc() targets unanchored two-trial case.

title: The cpaic statistical framework
text: cmaic() reweights IPD study effect-modifier distribution matches target population, using entropy-balancing weights wi=exp⁡(x̃⊤α)w_i = \exp(\tilde x_i^\top \alpha) x̃\tilde x_i centered effect modifiers (Phillippo et al. 2020). effective sample size ESS=(∑iwi)2/∑iwi2\mathrm{ESS} = (\sum_i w_i)^2 / \sum_i w_i^2. weighted within-study contrasts, bootstrap standard errors propagate weighting uncertainty, combined component model.
code: fit_maic <- cmaic(net_ipd, target = c(x1 = 0), effect_modifiers = "x1",                   n_boot = 100, seed = 1) effective_sample_size(fit_maic) #>       S3       S4  #> 207.4202 358.1461

title: The cpaic statistical framework
text: single IPD edge anchored common comparator, combining adjusted contrast aggregate comparator contrast component model exactly Bucher indirect comparison. component model generalizes network: many adjusted unadjusted contrasts combined simultaneously β̂=(X⊤WX)+X⊤Wd\hat\beta = (X^\top W X)^{+} X^\top W d, bridge supplies contrasts otherwise unavailable across disconnection. key assumption component effects (effect-modifier interactions) constant across sub-networks (Rücker et al. 2021).

title: The cpaic statistical framework
text: cmlnmr() places component structure inside multilevel network meta-regression: relative effect arm CβC\beta rather free per-treatment parameter, aggregate arms fitted integrating individual model study’s covariate distribution. Disconnected sub-networks share component parameters, network connected construction. four outcome families supported; survival uses proportional-hazards model flexible baseline (piecewise-exponential default, smooth M-spline), set cut_points baseline. Aggregate covariates integrated Gaussian copula whose correlation estimated individual patient data. component whose effect-modifier interaction informed aggregate data weakly identified relies regression prior.
code: # Requires cmdstanr; see ?cmlnmr. fit <- cmlnmr(ipd, agd, effect_modifiers = "x1", inactive = "Placebo",               family = "binomial") component_effects(fit)

title: The cpaic statistical framework
text: Additivity component effects (test additivity_test(); add interaction terms violated). Identifiability: disconnected network bridgeable cpaic_connectivity() reports identifiable = TRUE. Cross-population transportability effect modifiers (standard population-adjustment assumption), extended constancy component effects across sub-networks. Non-collapsibility: MAIC targets marginal effect STC conditional effect, noncollapsible measures answer slightly different questions.

title: Component-Based Population-Adjusted Indirect Comparison
text: Component-Based Population-Adjusted Indirect Comparison cpaic extends component network meta-analysis (cNMA) population-adjusted indirect comparison (PAIC), disconnected treatment network can reconnected shared treatment components , time, adjusted -study differences effect modifiers. Standard network meta-analysis needs connected network. network disconnected (common comparator links two sub-networks), analyzed directly. Two ideas solve half problem: Component NMA (Rücker et al. 2020) decomposes multi-component treatments additive component effects. sub-networks share components, component effects bridge gap; method uses aggregate data ignores effect-modifier imbalance. PAIC (STC, MAIC, ML-NMR) adjusts effect-modifier imbalance using individual patient data (IPD), assumes network already connected. cpaic composes : component structure supplies bridge, STC / MAIC / ML-NMR supply population adjustment.

title: Component-Based Population-Adjusted Indirect Comparison
text: different words happen spelled , conflating causes real confusion: Anchored PAIC (NICE sense): adjusted comparison runs common comparator arm within trial, rather comparing single arms. cmaic() cstc() anchored sense. Anchored cNMA (Rücker/Wigle sense): inactive component fixed zero component parameterization. Setting inactive = NULL gives unanchored parameterization Wigle & Béliveau (2022), every unit receives parameter anchor can misspecified. Bridging disconnected network shared components anchoring common comparator. identification additivity.

title: Component-Based Population-Adjusted Indirect Comparison
text: Frequentist core. cnma_bridge() (reconnect via components), cmaic() (component MAIC), cstc() (component anchored STC). Binary, continuous, count, survival outcomes. Bayesian cmlnmr() (component-additive ML-NMR), fitted cmdstanr. Binary, continuous, count, survival outcomes. research software active development. Read Limitations section using decision.

title: Component-Based Population-Adjusted Indirect Comparison
text: Reconnecting network shared components guarantee effects want estimable. relative effect uniquely estimable exactly contrast vector lies row space component design matrix X = B C (Wigle et al. 2026). Full column rank X sufficient necessary, rank-deficient network can still identify useful cross-sub-network contrasts. matters engines otherwise hand back confident-looking number contrast carries information: frequentist fit Moore-Penrose pseudoinverse, Bayesian fit prior. cpaic checks every contrast returns NA instead.
code: estimable_effects(net)     # which relative effects are identified? cpaic_connectivity(net)    # rank, null space, bridging components

title: Component-Based Population-Adjusted Indirect Comparison
text: cpaic builds netmeta (cNMA), maicplus (MAIC weights), multinma (ML-NMR numerical integration), CRAN. cmlnmr() additionally needs cmdstanr.
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("choxos/cpaic")

title: Component-Based Population-Adjusted Indirect Comparison
text: Bayesian model, relative effects population-specific: model contains component effect-modifier interactions, theta_t(x) = C_t' (beta + Gamma x). must name target population. relative_effects(), league_table(), component_effects(), estimable_effects(), additivity_test(), effective_sample_size(), forest() plot() summarize visualize fit.
code: library(cpaic)  # A disconnected network: sub-network {Placebo, A, B} and isolated # sub-network {A+B, A+B+C, A+B+D}, bridged by the shared components A and B. net <- cpaic_network(cpaic_bin_agd, ipd = cpaic_bin_ipd, sm = "OR",                      family = "binomial", ipd_covariates = "x1",                      inactive = "Placebo")  # Which effects can this network actually identify? cpaic_connectivity(net) estimable_effects(net)  # 1. Connect only (aggregate component NMA) cnma_bridge(net)  # 2. Connect and population-adjust via anchored STC cstc(net, target = c(x1 = 0), effect_modifiers = "x1")  # 3. Connect and population-adjust via anchored MAIC cmaic(net, target = c(x1 = 0), effect_modifiers = "x1") fit <- cmlnmr(ipd, agd, effect_modifiers = "x1", inactive = "Placebo") relative_effects(fit, newdata = data.frame(x1 = 0.3))   # effects at x1 = 0.3 component_effects(fit, newdata = data.frame(x1 = 0.3))

title: Component-Based Population-Adjusted Indirect Comparison
text: Read trusting result. bridging assumption untestable. Reconnecting disconnected network requires component effects (, population adjustment, component effect-modifier interactions) constant across sub-networks. construction cross-gap evidence test . additivity_test() reports fit additive model within observed evidence; large p-value licence bridge. IPD edges adjusted. cmaic() cstc() adjust edges IPD; aggregate contrasts enter bridge published, study populations. pooled result therefore fully adjusted single target retained aggregate effects transportable. Estimands differ across methods. cstc() returns conditional effect target covariate values; cmaic() returns marginal weighted effect target population. non-collapsible scales (odds ratios, hazard ratios) differ even correct. Survival approximate. aggregate survival likelihood approximates expected events person-time times mean hazard, biased upward higher-hazard individuals leave risk set earlier. "mspline" baseline step baseline whose interval heights smoothed M-spline; continuous-time integrated M-spline likelihood multinma. Right-censoring . cmlnmr() common-effect model. -study heterogeneity.

title: Component-Based Population-Adjusted Indirect Comparison

Bayesian unanchored additive models component network meta-analysis. Statistics Medicine 41(22):4444-4466. Wigle , Béliveau , Nikolakopoulou , Lin L (2026). Creating treatment component hierarchies component network meta-analysis. Efthimiou O, et al. (2022). Bayesian model combining aggregate individual participant data component network meta-analysis. Statistics Medicine 41(14):2586-2606. Phillippo DM, et al. (2020). Multilevel network meta-regression population-adjusted treatment comparisons. JRSS 183(3):1189-1210.

what: Fit statistics for the additive component model — additivity_test
title: Fit statistics for the additive component model — additivity_test
text: Returns Cochran Q statistics netmeta::discomb().

title: Fit statistics for the additive component model — additivity_test
code: additivity_test(object, ...)

title: Fit statistics for the additive component model — additivity_test
text: object cpaic_bridge cpaic_fit object. ... Unused.

title: Fit statistics for the additive component model — additivity_test
text: one-row data frame Q, df, pval (total additive-model lack fit) Q.diff, df.diff, pval.diff (nested additivity test, NA disconnected network).

title: Fit statistics for the additive component model — additivity_test
text: Two different statistics reported, one tests additivity: Q (Q.additive) total lack fit additive component model, pooling ordinary heterogeneity/inconsistency failure additivity. test additivity. Q.diff = Q.additive - Q.standard nested test additivity restrictions , statistic read. exists standard (non-additive) NMA also estimable, .e. connected network; disconnected network NA. Neither statistic can test assumption actually bridges disconnected network, namely component effects (, population adjustment, component x effect-modifier interactions) constant across sub-networks. construction cross-gap evidence test . large p-value therefore licence bridge; assumption must defended clinical grounds (Veroniki et al. 2026).

what: Build a component-coded treatment-by-component matrix — build_C_matrix
title: Build a component-coded treatment-by-component matrix — build_C_matrix
text: Splits multi-component treatment labels (e.g. "+ B") sep.comps returns binary treatment--component design matrix C, C[t, c] = 1 treatment t contains component c. inactive component (e.g. placebo) represented -zero row, matching convention netmeta::netcomb().

title: Build a component-coded treatment-by-component matrix — build_C_matrix
code: build_C_matrix(treatments, sep.comps = "+", inactive = NULL)

title: Build a component-coded treatment-by-component matrix — build_C_matrix
text: treatments Character vector (unique) treatment labels. sep.comps Single character separating components treatment label. Default "+". inactive Optional name inactive/reference treatment component (mapped zero row / dropped column).

title: Build a component-coded treatment-by-component matrix — build_C_matrix
text: binary matrix one row per treatment one column per component (treatments row names, components column names).

title: Build a component-coded treatment-by-component matrix — build_C_matrix
code: build_C_matrix(c("A", "B", "A + B", "placebo"), inactive = "placebo") #>         A B #> A       1 0 #> B       0 1 #> A + B   1 1 #> placebo 0 0

title: Component matching-adjusted indirect comparison (cMAIC) — cmaic
text: Anchored MAIC generalized (possibly disconnected) component network. IPD study reweighted maicplus::estimate_weights() effect-modifier distribution matches common target population; resulting population-adjusted within-study contrasts (bootstrap standard errors propagate weighting uncertainty) replace corresponding unadjusted aggregate contrasts. Finally cnma_bridge() combines contrasts additive component model, yielding relative effects connected across sub-networks adjusted target population.

title: Component matching-adjusted indirect comparison (cMAIC) — cmaic
code: cmaic(   network,   target,   effect_modifiers = NULL,   target_sd = NULL,   n_boot = 500,   seed = NULL,   common = FALSE,   random = TRUE )

title: Component matching-adjusted indirect comparison (cMAIC) — cmaic
text: network cpaic_network() object includes IPD. target Named numeric vector (one-row data frame / list) giving target-population means effect modifiers. effect_modifiers Character vector covariates match (defaults IPD covariates). Matching effect modifiers anchored-MAIC convention. target_sd Optional named numeric vector target standard deviations; supplied, second moments matched well. n_boot Number bootstrap resamples adjusted-contrast standard errors. Default 500. seed Optional RNG seed reproducible bootstrap. common, random Passed cnma_bridge().

title: Component matching-adjusted indirect comparison (cMAIC) — cmaic
text: object class cpaic_maic (also inheriting cpaic_bridge structure via $bridge), bridged fit, per-study effective sample sizes, target population.

title: Component matching-adjusted indirect comparison (cMAIC) — cmaic
code: net <- cpaic_network(cpaic_bin_agd, ipd = cpaic_bin_ipd, sm = "OR",                      family = "binomial", ipd_covariates = "x1",                      inactive = "Placebo") # \donttest{ fit <- cmaic(net, target = c(x1 = 0), effect_modifiers = "x1",              n_boot = 100, seed = 1) relative_effects(fit) #> Relative effects (OR, back-transformed) #>  treatment comparator estimate    se lower  upper     z     p #>          A    Placebo    1.649 0.401 0.752  3.615 1.248 0.212 #>        A+B    Placebo    2.460 0.567 0.810  7.466 1.589 0.112 #>      A+B+C    Placebo    4.941 0.672 1.323 18.448 2.377 0.017 #>      A+B+D    Placebo    5.324 0.666 1.443 19.647 2.510 0.012 #>          B    Placebo    1.492 0.401 0.680  3.271 0.999 0.318 effective_sample_size(fit) #>       S3       S4  #> 207.4202 358.1461  # }

title: Component-additive multilevel network meta-regression (ML-NMR) — cmlnmr
text: Bayesian flagship cpaic. relative effect every treatment sum component effects, estimated jointly individual patient data (IPD) aggregate data (AgD). Aggregate arms fitted integrating individual-level model study's covariate distribution, averaging outcome natural scale (link scale). disconnected sub-networks share component parameters, network connected construction.

title: Component-additive multilevel network meta-regression (ML-NMR) — cmlnmr
code: cmlnmr(   ipd,   agd,   effect_modifiers,   inactive = NULL,   sep.comps = "+",   family = "binomial",   margins = NULL,   study = ".study",   trt = ".trt",   outcome = ".y",   time = ".time",   exposure = ".exposure",   r = "r",   n = "n",   E = "E",   se = "se",   cut_points = NULL,   interval = ".interval",   baseline = c("piecewise", "mspline"),   n_basis = 6L,   cor = NULL,   n_int = 64L,   prior_intercept_sd = 10,   prior_beta_sd = 10,   prior_reg_sd = 2.5,   chains = 4L,   iter_warmup = 500L,   iter_sampling = 500L,   seed = NULL,   ... )

title: Component-additive multilevel network meta-regression (ML-NMR) — cmlnmr
text: ipd Individual patient data (one row per patient). agd Aggregate data (one row per arm) per-study covariate summaries x_mean (x_sd normal margins) effect modifier x. effect_modifiers Character vector effect-modifier names. inactive, sep.comps Component coding (see cpaic_network()). inactive = NULL gives unanchored component parameterization, every unit receives parameter (Wigle & Béliveau 2022). family One "binomial", "gaussian", "poisson", "survival". margins Optional named character vector giving integration margin effect modifier: "normal" "bernoulli". Defaults Bernoulli 0/1 covariates normal otherwise. study, trt Column names (ipd agd). outcome IPD outcome column: 0/1 (binomial), numeric (gaussian), count (poisson), event indicator survival. time, exposure IPD time / exposure column (survival, poisson). r, n, E, se Aggregate columns: events r, sample size n (binomial), exposure E (poisson/survival), mean outcome standard error se (gaussian). cut_points Survival : interior interval boundaries piecewise-constant baseline. NULL (default) gives exponential model; e.g. c(6, 12) gives three intervals. interval Survival : name aggregate interval-index column (values 1..K) required cut_points supplied. baseline Survival baseline hazard: "piecewise" (default, free step heights) "mspline" (step heights smoothed M-spline evaluated interval midpoints; see Survival section). n_basis Number M-spline basis functions baseline = "mspline" (must <= number intervals). cor Optional covariate correlation matrix Gaussian-copula integration. Must positive-definite correlation matrix (unit diagonal). Defaults within-study IPD correlation. n_int Integration points per aggregate arm (ignored gaussian, exact covariate means). prior_intercept_sd, prior_beta_sd, prior_reg_sd Prior SDs. chains, iter_warmup, iter_sampling, seed Passed cmdstanr. ... Passed cmdstanr sampler (e.g. adapt_delta).

title: Component-additive multilevel network meta-regression (ML-NMR) — cmlnmr
text: object class cpaic_mlnmr cmdstanr fit, component design, tidy table component effects.

title: Component-additive multilevel network meta-regression (ML-NMR) — cmlnmr
text: model includes component x effect-modifier interactions gamma, treatment effect population-specific: $$\theta_t(x) = C_t' (\beta + \Gamma x).$$ component main effects beta effects covariate origin (x = 0) population-adjusted quantity. Use newdata relative_effects() / component_effects() obtain effects named target population. Supported families: "binomial" (logit), "gaussian" (identity), "poisson" (log), "survival".

title: Component-additive multilevel network meta-regression (ML-NMR) — cmlnmr
text: Aggregate covariates integrated Sobol' quasi-Monte-Carlo points coupled Gaussian copula, whose correlation pooled within IPD studies Fisher z scale (supplied via cor). covariate pushed marginal inverse CDF: margins may "normal" (using x_mean x_sd) "bernoulli" (using x_mean prevalence). Margins default Bernoulli covariates 0/1 IPD normal otherwise; normal margin binary covariate integrate population occur.

title: Component-additive multilevel network meta-regression (ML-NMR) — cmlnmr
text: Survival uses proportional-hazards model piecewise-constant baseline log-hazard cut_points grid (cut_points = NULL gives exponential model). baseline = "mspline" interval heights smoothed M-spline basis evaluated interval midpoints. piecewise-exponential model smoothed step baseline; continuous-time integrated M-spline survival likelihood multinma, uses M-spline hazard basis integrated (-spline) cumulative hazard supports left-, interval-censoring delayed entry. cpaic handles right-censoring . Aggregate survival data supplied events person-time per arm per interval, expected events approximated person-time x mean hazard. approximation even without censoring, higher-hazard individuals leave risk set earlier, baseline covariate distribution describe covariate distribution accumulated person-time. two-group example (hazards 0.1 0.4, 50:50, follow-t = 10) overstates expected events 36%. Narrow intervals reduce bias; supply IPD, interval-specific risk-set covariate summaries, accuracy matters.

title: Component-additive multilevel network meta-regression (ML-NMR) — cmlnmr
text: relative effect uniquely estimable component contrast lies row space within-study component design (Wigle et al. 2026); relative_effects() returns NA otherwise rather prior-driven number. Note checks identification beta; component x effect-modifier interaction additionally identified covariate variation contrasts involve , interactions informed aggregate arms weakly identified (prior_reg_sd regularizes).

title: Component-additive multilevel network meta-regression (ML-NMR) — cmlnmr
text: Phillippo DM, Dias S, Ades AE, et al. (2020). Multilevel network meta-regression population-adjusted treatment comparisons. JRSS , 183(3), 1189–1210. Wigle , Beliveau , Nikolakopoulou , Lin L (2026). Creating Treatment Component Hierarchies Component Network Meta-Analysis.

title: Component-additive multilevel network meta-regression (ML-NMR) — cmlnmr
code: if (FALSE) { # requireNamespace("cmdstanr", quietly = TRUE) && !inherits(try(cmdstanr::cmdstan_path(), silent = TRUE), "try-error") # \donttest{ ipd <- data.frame(.study = "S1",                   .trt = rep(c("Placebo", "A"), each = 100),                   .y = rbinom(200, 1, 0.5), x1 = rnorm(200)) agd <- data.frame(.study = "S2", .trt = c("Placebo", "A+B"),                   r = c(40, 55), n = c(100, 100),                   x1_mean = c(0.2, 0.2), x1_sd = c(1, 1)) fit <- cmlnmr(ipd, agd, effect_modifiers = "x1", inactive = "Placebo",               chains = 2, iter_warmup = 200, iter_sampling = 200) # Effects in a named target population (x1 = 0.2), not at the origin: relative_effects(fit, newdata = data.frame(x1 = 0.2)) # } }

what: Reconnect a network through its additive component structure — cnma_bridge
title: Reconnect a network through its additive component structure — cnma_bridge
text: Fits additive component network meta-analysis (cNMA) model Rücker et al. (2020) aggregate contrast data, using netmeta::discomb(). network disconnected sub-networks share components, additive model estimates component effects reconstructs relative effects across sub-networks. "connect first" step; population adjustment layered cmaic() / cstc(), replace unadjusted contrasts adjusted ones calling function.

title: Reconnect a network through its additive component structure — cnma_bridge
code: cnma_bridge(network, common = FALSE, random = TRUE, ...)

title: Reconnect a network through its additive component structure — cnma_bridge
text: network cpaic_network() object. common, random Fit common- /random-effects models. ... Additional arguments passed netmeta::discomb() (e.g. tau.preset).

title: Reconnect a network through its additive component structure — cnma_bridge
text: object class cpaic_bridge wrapping netmeta::discomb() fit, tidied component treatment effects.

title: Reconnect a network through its additive component structure — cnma_bridge
text: Estimability checked per contrast, single global rank test: relative effect uniquely estimable contrast vector lies row space component design matrix X = B C (Wigle et al. 2026). rank-deficient network therefore rejected outright; contrasts remain estimable still reported, returned NA rather pseudoinverse artefacts. See estimable_effects().

title: Reconnect a network through its additive component structure — cnma_bridge
text: Rücker G, Petropoulou M, Schwarzer G (2020). Network meta-analysis multicomponent interventions. Biometrical Journal, 62(3), 808–821. Wigle , Beliveau , Nikolakopoulou , Lin L (2026). Creating Treatment Component Hierarchies Component Network Meta-Analysis.

what: cpaic: Component-Based Population-Adjusted Indirect Comparison — cpaic-package
title: cpaic: Component-Based Population-Adjusted Indirect Comparison — cpaic-package
text: Extends component network meta-analysis (cNMA) population-adjusted indirect comparison (PAIC) methods disconnected treatment networks can reconnected adjusted -study differences effect modifiers. additive component structure cNMA (Ruecker et al. 2020 doi:10.1002/bimj.201800167 ) used bridge otherwise disconnected sub-networks share treatment components, anchored simulated treatment comparison (STC), matching-adjusted indirect comparison (MAIC), multilevel network meta-regression (ML-NMR; Phillippo et al. 2020 doi:10.1111/rssa.12579 ) provide population adjustment using individual patient data. Supports binary, continuous, count, time--event outcomes. Builds 'netmeta', 'maicplus', 'multinma' packages.

title: cpaic: Component-Based Population-Adjusted Indirect Comparison — cpaic-package
text: Maintainer: Ahmad Sofi-Mahmudi ahmad.pub@gmail.com (ORCID) Authors: Ahmad Sofi-Mahmudi ahmad.pub@gmail.com (ORCID)

what: Example disconnected component network: aggregate contrasts — cpaic_bin_agd
title: Example disconnected component network: aggregate contrasts — cpaic_bin_agd
text: constructed binary-outcome network used throughout examples tests. disconnected: sub-network 1 (Placebo, , B) anchored placebo, sub-network 2 (+B, +B+C, +B+D) shares treatment sub-network 1. shared components B bridge two, components C D identified within sub-network 2.

title: Example disconnected component network: aggregate contrasts — cpaic_bin_agd
code: cpaic_bin_agd

title: Example disconnected component network: aggregate contrasts — cpaic_bin_agd
text: data frame 5 rows 5 columns: studlab Study label. treat1, treat2 Treatments compared (components joined "+"). TE Log odds ratio treat1 versus treat2. seTE Standard error TE. attribute "truth" holds data-generating component log-odds ratios.

title: Example disconnected component network: aggregate contrasts — cpaic_bin_agd
text: Studies S3 S4 also individual patient data (cpaic_bin_ipd); rows unadjusted contrasts, cmaic() / cstc() replace population-adjusted versions.

title: Example disconnected component network: individual patient data — cpaic_bin_ipd

code: cpaic_bin_ipd

what: Assess connectivity and component-bridge identifiability of a network — cpaic_connectivity
title: Assess connectivity and component-bridge identifiability of a network — cpaic_connectivity
text: Reports whether treatment network connected , disconnected network, relative effects additive component structure makes estimable.

title: Assess connectivity and component-bridge identifiability of a network — cpaic_connectivity
code: cpaic_connectivity(network, tol = 1e-08)

title: Assess connectivity and component-bridge identifiability of a network — cpaic_connectivity
text: network cpaic_network() object. tol Numerical tolerance rank null-space computations.

title: Assess connectivity and component-bridge identifiability of a network — cpaic_connectivity
text: object class cpaic_connectivity: list connected (logical), n_subnetworks, subnetworks (list treatment-label vectors), bridging_components (components shared across sub-networks), rank n_components, identifiable (logical: rank == n_components), null_space, estimable_components, estimable (data frame estimable relative effects versus reference), B/C/X matrices.

title: Assess connectivity and component-bridge identifiability of a network — cpaic_connectivity
text: Two distinct questions answered, (Wigle et al. 2026): component effects identified? Yes component design matrix X = B C full column rank (rank(X) equal n_components). Reported identifiable. particular relative effect estimable? Yes contrast vector lies row space X. Full column rank sufficient necessary, rank-deficient network can still identify useful cross-sub-network contrasts. Reported per treatment estimable (see estimable_effects()).

title: Assess connectivity and component-bridge identifiability of a network — cpaic_connectivity
text: Wigle , Beliveau , Nikolakopoulou , Lin L (2026). Creating Treatment Component Hierarchies Component Network Meta-Analysis.

title: Assess connectivity and component-bridge identifiability of a network — cpaic_connectivity
code: net <- cpaic_network(cpaic_bin_agd, sm = "OR", inactive = "Placebo") cpaic_connectivity(net) #> cpaic connectivity #>   Connected network: FALSE #>   Sub-networks:      2 #>     [1] 3 treatments #>     [2] 3 treatments #>   Bridging components: A, B #>   Component design:  rank(X) = 4 / 4 components -> all component effects identified #>   Estimable effects: 5 / 5 vs Placebo

what: Set up a (possibly disconnected) component network for cpaic — cpaic_network
title: Set up a (possibly disconnected) component network for cpaic — cpaic_network
text: Builds network object consumed cnma_bridge(), cmaic(), cstc() (Phase 2) cmlnmr(). Aggregate data supplied contrast level (one row per pairwise comparison: treat1 vs treat2, treatment effect TE standard error seTE), matching input convention netmeta::discomb(). Individual patient data (IPD) optional used population-adjusted methods replace study's unadjusted contrast adjusted one.

title: Set up a (possibly disconnected) component network for cpaic — cpaic_network
code: cpaic_network(   agd,   ipd = NULL,   treat1 = "treat1",   treat2 = "treat2",   TE = "TE",   seTE = "seTE",   studlab = "studlab",   sm,   inactive = NULL,   sep.comps = "+",   reference = NULL,   family = NULL,   ipd_study = ".study",   ipd_trt = ".trt",   ipd_outcome = ".y",   ipd_time = NULL,   ipd_status = NULL,   ipd_exposure = NULL,   ipd_covariates = NULL )

title: Set up a (possibly disconnected) component network for cpaic — cpaic_network
text: agd Aggregate (contrast-level) data frame. ipd Optional individual patient data frame (one row per patient). Must contain study column, treatment column, outcome, effect-modifier / prognostic covariates. treat1, treat2, TE, seTE, studlab Column names agd. sm Summary measure (e.g. "", "RR", "MD", "HR"), passed netmeta::discomb(). inactive Name inactive component / reference (e.g. "placebo"). sep.comps Component separator treatment labels. Default "+". reference Reference treatment reported relative effects. Defaults inactive available, otherwise first treatment. family Outcome family IPD model, one "binomial", "gaussian", "poisson", "survival" (required ipd given). ipd_study, ipd_trt, ipd_outcome Column names ipd. survival outcomes use ipd_time/ipd_status instead (alongside) ipd_outcome; Poisson rates optional ipd_exposure offset. ipd_time, ipd_status Time event-indicator column names ipd (survival family). ipd_exposure Optional exposure/person-time column ipd (Poisson family); used log offset. ipd_covariates Character vector covariate column names ipd (candidate effect modifiers / prognostic factors).

title: Set up a (possibly disconnected) component network for cpaic — cpaic_network
text: object class cpaic_network: list elements agd, ipd, treatments, components, C.matrix, sm, inactive, reference, sep.comps, family, column-name mappings.

title: Set up a (possibly disconnected) component network for cpaic — cpaic_network
text: Treatment labels encode components via sep.comps (e.g. "+ B"), sub-networks sharing components can bridged.

title: Set up a (possibly disconnected) component network for cpaic — cpaic_network
code: # Aggregate-only, disconnected network net <- cpaic_network(cpaic_bin_agd, sm = "OR", inactive = "Placebo") net #> cpaic component network #>   Summary measure:   OR #>   Treatments:        6 #>   Components:        4 (A, B, C, D) #>   AgD comparisons:   5 #>   Reference:         Placebo #>   Inactive:          Placebo #>   IPD studies:        none #>   Connected:         FALSE | components bridgeable: TRUE  # With individual patient data for the population-adjusted methods net_ipd <- cpaic_network(cpaic_bin_agd, ipd = cpaic_bin_ipd, sm = "OR",                          family = "binomial", ipd_covariates = "x1",                          inactive = "Placebo")

title: Component simulated treatment comparison (cSTC) — cstc
text: Anchored STC generalized (possibly disconnected) component network. IPD study outcome regression fitted treatment main effects, prognostic main effects, treatment--effect-modifier interactions; effect modifiers centered common target population treatment coefficient anchored, population-adjusted contrast population. adjusted contrasts replace corresponding unadjusted aggregate contrasts cnma_bridge() combines additive component model.

title: Component simulated treatment comparison (cSTC) — cstc
code: cstc(   network,   target,   effect_modifiers = NULL,   prognostics = NULL,   common = FALSE,   random = TRUE )

title: Component simulated treatment comparison (cSTC) — cstc
text: network cpaic_network() object includes IPD. target Named numeric vector (list / one-row data frame) target-population means effect modifiers. effect_modifiers Covariates interact treatment (centered target). Defaults IPD covariates. prognostics Covariates included main effects . Defaults effect modifiers (enters main effect + interaction). common, random Passed cnma_bridge().

title: Component simulated treatment comparison (cSTC) — cstc
text: object class cpaic_stc (cpaic_fit).

title: Component simulated treatment comparison (cSTC) — cstc
text: Unlike cmaic() (reweighting) regression-adjustment route. reported treatment coefficient conditional effect target effect-modifier means (marginal standardization); collapsible measures two coincide. implemented natively mlumr::stc() targets unanchored two-trial case; link standard-error machinery adapted package.

title: Component simulated treatment comparison (cSTC) — cstc
code: net <- cpaic_network(cpaic_bin_agd, ipd = cpaic_bin_ipd, sm = "OR",                      family = "binomial", ipd_covariates = "x1",                      inactive = "Placebo") fit <- cstc(net, target = c(x1 = 0), effect_modifiers = "x1") relative_effects(fit) #> Relative effects (OR, back-transformed) #>  treatment comparator estimate    se lower  upper     z     p #>          A    Placebo    1.649 0.256 0.998  2.725 1.951 0.051 #>        A+B    Placebo    2.460 0.363 1.209  5.005 2.483 0.013 #>      A+B+C    Placebo    4.014 0.435 1.711  9.416 3.194 0.001 #>      A+B+D    Placebo    4.669 0.430 2.009 10.850 3.582 0.000 #>          B    Placebo    1.492 0.256 0.903  2.466 1.560 0.119 additivity_test(fit) #> Additive component model: fit statistics #>   Total lack of fit (Q.additive): Q = 2.669, df = 1, p = 0.102 #>   Additivity restrictions (Q.diff): not available -- no standard NMA #>     is estimable on a disconnected network. #>   Note: neither statistic tests whether component effects are constant #>   ACROSS sub-networks, which is the assumption that bridges the gap. #>   That assumption is untestable from the data and must be justified #>   clinically.

what: Which relative effects of a component network are uniquely estimable? — estimable_effects
title: Which relative effects of a component network are uniquely estimable? — estimable_effects
text: additive component model identifies relative effect theta_i - theta_j = (C_i - C_j)' beta contrast vector C_i - C_j lies row space design matrix X = B C (Wigle et al. 2026). Full column rank X (rank equal number components) sufficient every contrast estimable, necessary: disconnected, rank-deficient component network can still identify many cross-sub-network treatment contrasts.

title: Which relative effects of a component network are uniquely estimable? — estimable_effects
text: Checking matters engines otherwise return finite-looking answer contrast carries information: frequentist weighted least squares Moore-Penrose pseudoinverse, Bayesian model prior.

title: Which relative effects of a component network are uniquely estimable? — estimable_effects
text: Wigle , Beliveau , Nikolakopoulou , Lin L (2026). Creating Treatment Component Hierarchies Component Network Meta-Analysis.

title: Relative treatment effects from a cpaic fit — relative_effects
text: object fitted cpaic object (cpaic_bridge, cpaic_maic, cpaic_stc, cpaic_mlnmr). reference Reference treatment. Defaults network reference. all_contrasts TRUE, return pairwise comparisons instead versus reference. backtransf TRUE (default) back-transform log-scale measures (/RR/HR/...) exponentiating. level Confidence level intervals. Default 0.95. newdata cmlnmr() fits: one-row data frame giving target population's effect-modifier values. Required model effect modifiers. ... Unused.

title: Relative treatment effects from a cpaic fit — relative_effects
text: data frame columns treatment, comparator, estimate, se (link scale), lower, upper, z/p frequentist fits. cmlnmr() (Bayesian) fits intervals credible intervals final column pr_gt0, posterior probability effect (link scale) exceeds zero, instead z/p.

title: Relative treatment effects from a cpaic fit — relative_effects
text: Relative effects component design uniquely identify (contrast vector lies outside row space X = B C) returned NA rather pseudoinverse prior-driven artefacts. See estimable_effects(). cmlnmr() fits model contains component x effect-modifier interactions, relative effects population-specific: theta_t(x) = C_t' (beta + gamma x). must name target population newdata; population-free relative effect.
